font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">Ash woke up... another nightmare, if you can call a memory a nightmare.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">He rubbed his eyes and glanced at his wrist.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">Red numbers flooded his vision,<br /><del style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #ec6f6f; color: #111111; font-style: inherit !important; font-weight: inherit !important; line-height: 26px !important;">3:00</del><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">three</ins>am.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;">He rolled over and went back to sleep.</div></div><h4 style="border-bottom: 2px solid rgb(238, 238, 238); color: #353e34; font-size: 16px; line-height: 22px; margin: 40px 0px 10px; padding-bottom: 5px;">Closing Comments</h4><div style="margin-top: 10px;">Scrib academy has a great article on filtering-what it is, why you don't want it, and how to fix it.</div><div style="margin-top: 10px;">you want to make sure you don't have to many sentence starts in a row identical. more then two and the reader will be bored.</div><div style="margin-top: 10px;">Try to describe whats happening not tell it. H<u>e slammed the door angrily!</u>--The door rebounded off the wall with a thud. See, we know he did it angrily, but it's more interesting. It also helps fix the, he did this, he did that, he did this other thing, that makes a story boring.</div><div style="margin-top: 10px;"><a href="http://hubpages.com/literature/The-Secret-of-How-to-Write-a-Book-and-Get-It-Published-Part-2" style="color: #c70c14; outline: 0px; text-decoration: none;">http://hubpages.com/literature/The-Secret-of-How-to-Write-a-Book-and-Get-It-Published-Part-2</a></div><div style="margin-top: 10px;">the above site can explain better than I why you don't want all those pronoun starts and how to fix them, basically you change the subject of the sentence.</div><div style="margin-top: 10px;">To correct the pronoun starts put the noun first. example--He got in a car.--fixed--The car he got in was red.---fixed better -- The red car he rode in smelled of cigarettes. In the last example the sentence went from passive to active with the removal of was got in was tightened to rode and interesting sensory detail was added with a smell.</div><div style="margin-top: 10px;">go through the entire thing and replace all the was, were. could, would, and put in the correct tenses. by that I mean was and were should denote tense not be used because you cant think of a correct verb. but that being said sometimes word have a tense like--could hear=heard was wearing=wore were going=gone was running=ran etc lots of words have tenses so use then when applicable. I make a list of my common mistakes and when I think I'm done with a piece I run a scan for them.</div><div style="margin-top: 10px;">replacing generic words for more descriptive ones will add depth. common generic words are--Look, watch, came, went, took, walk, basically action words. instead of having the charter walk have them stride pace amble march slink limp etc picture in your head HOW they do the action and use that word.</div><div style="margin-top: 10px;">replacing the vague words will add clarity and flow. common vague words are --Area people everyone them it they stuff someone something everything things all some</div><div style="margin-top: 10px;">it isn't unusual to have to do major rewrites, sometimes even more than once so don't let this discourage you. this is a solid first draft that just requires fleshing out and tightening.</div><div style="margin-top: 10px;">Don't rush through the scenes, take your time and write them so we can see them.</div><div style="margin-top: 10px;">Sorry if I offended, it wasn't my intent. Writing is so personal that any criticism can feel like an attack. all these things I mention are minor tweaks easily done. you've already done the hard part--the good idea. now polish till it shines.</div><div style="margin-top: 10px;">If you aren't sure if you have to many pronoun starts try a software editor.&nbsp;&nbsp;prowriting aid has a paid addin for Microsoft Word and google docs but they have a free version too. autocrit is good but costs alot more money. I highly recommend you try the free version of prowriting aid</div><div style="margin-top: 10px;">S.</div></div></div><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq" style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: Verdana, Geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 15.4px; line-height: 19.6px;"><div><br /></div></blockquote><h4 style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: Verdana, Geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 15.4px; line-height: 19.6px; margin: 0px; position: relative;">By&nbsp;<a href="https://plus.google.com/+DarrellWolfe/posts" style="color: #149adf; text-decoration: none;" target="_blank">Darrell Wolfe</a></h4><blockquote class="tr_bq" style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: Verdana, Geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 15.4px; line-height: 19.6px;"><i>Storyteller | Creative | <a href="http://www.16personalities.com/infj-personality" style="color: #149adf; text-decoration: none;" target="_blank">INFJ</a>&nbsp;|&nbsp;<a href="https://www.gallupstrengthscenter.com/Purchase/en-US/Product?Path=Clifton%20StrengthsFinder" style="color: #149adf; text-decoration: none;" target="_blank">Intellection</a>&nbsp;|&nbsp;Ideation | Input | Learner | Achiever | Multipotentialite</i></blockquote><ul><li><i>See my full about page and disclosures: <a href="http://authorfun.blogspot.com/p/what-is-this.html" target="_blank">Click Here</a>.&nbsp;</i></li><li><i>Also check out my <a href="http://authorfun.blogspot.com/p/affiliate-store.html" target="_blank">Resources and Suggestions </a>for writers (<a href="http://authorfun.blogspot.com/p/affiliate-store.html" target="_blank">here</a>).&nbsp;</i></li><li><i>To Subscribe to the <a href="http://feeds.feedburner.com/AuthorFun" target="_blank">RSS: Click Here</a></i></li><li><i>For Non-Fiction writing by <a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/" target="_blank">Darrell Wolfe: Click Here</a></i></li></ul><ul></ul>
